steps.title: Understand the underlined claim
steps.content:
The underlined sentence argues that grid-cell-like behavior in neural networks has less to do with resemblance to biological brains and more to do with programmed rules. A supporting finding must show that the programming — not brain-like structure — explains the behavior.
  • "The rules that allow for networks to exhibit behaviors like those of grid cells have no equivalent in the function of biological brains.": If the rules enabling grid-cell behavior have no biological counterpart, then the behavior clearly comes from programming, not from any true resemblance to brains. This directly supports the claim. ✓
  • "The networks that do not exhibit behaviors like those of grid cells were nonetheless programmed with rules that had proven useful in earlier neural-network studies.": This concerns networks that don't exhibit grid-cell behavior and says nothing about whether the rules of those that do have a biological basis. ✗
  • "Neural networks can often accomplish tasks that biological brains do, but they are typically programmed with rules to model multiple types of brain cells simultaneously.": Modeling multiple cell types is unrelated to the claim about programmed rules vs. brain resemblance for grid-cell behavior specifically. ✗
  • "Once a neural network is programmed, it is trained on certain tasks to see if it can independently arrive at processes that are similar to those performed by biological brains.": This describes a training process but doesn't address whether the grid-cell-like behavior originates from programming or from genuine brain similarity. ✗

steps.title: Confirm the match
steps.content: The rules that allow for networks to exhibit behaviors like those of grid cells have no equivalent in the function of biological brains. establishes that the rules producing grid-cell-like behavior have no biological equivalent, proving the behavior stems from programming rather than brain resemblance. That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
